ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a sourdough bread class (FOODON_03302397) but no specific class for cheese-topped sourdough. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and similar products; no specific code for cheesy sourdough exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 covers bread as a substance; no specific code for cheesy sourdough. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one piece of golden cheesy sourdough bread, estimated at 120 g, as served at Kazo Premium, Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cheese bread, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18963) adapted with sourdough fermentation profile
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for golden cheesy sourdough exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheese bread (NDB 18963) and USDA sourdough bread entries, weighted to reflect a cheese-topped sourdough product. Energy, macros, sodium, and saturated fat are adjusted upward to account for the cheese topping. This is an estimated composite; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Sourdough wheat bread GI — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for golden cheesy sourdough. GI of 54 is taken from sourdough wheat bread (GI=54, low category) as reported in the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care), accessed via the University of Sydney GI Database. The addition of cheese does not substantially raise GI; fat and protein from cheese may modestly lower postprandial glucose response. GL per serving calculated as (54 × 41.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 26.6 g, using 120 g serving size and 41.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~624 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of golden cheesy sourdough due to its high sodium content (~624 mg per serving), which represents approximately 27% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; portion control and pairing with low-sodium foods is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 54) and high carbohydrate load per serving (~49 g carbohydrate per piece)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume golden cheesy sourdough in moderated portions; while sourdough fermentation lowers GI to approximately 54 (low-medium boundary), the glycemic load per serving is high (~26.6 g), and blood glucose monitoring after consumption is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese (~5.2 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit golden cheesy sourdough intake due to its saturated fat content (~6.2 g per serving) and high sodium (~624 mg per serving), both of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed in excess.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Golden Cheesy Sourdough is a baked artisan bread product served as a single piece at Kazo Premium, a café-style restaurant located at Funan Mall in Singapore. The dish consists of a thick slice or portion of sourdough bread — made from a naturally fermented dough using wild yeast and lactic acid bacteria — topped generously with melted cheese, giving it a golden, crispy, and savory finish. The sourdough base contributes a characteristic mild tanginess and chewy crumb texture, while the cheese topping adds richness, umami depth, and a slightly salty flavour. The bread is typically baked or toasted until the cheese is fully melted and lightly browned. Nutritionally, one piece provides approximately 348 kcal, 11.4 g protein, 11.8 g fat (of which ~6.2 g is saturated), and ~49 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notable at approximately 624 mg per serving. The glycemic index of sourdough bread is approximately 54 (low-medium), making it a relatively better choice than standard white bread for blood glucose management, though the glycemic load per serving remains high. Clinically, this food is of moderate concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its carbohydrate load, and of high concern for those with hypertension due to its sodium content. The cheese contributes calcium (~156 mg per serving) and vitamin B12. This product is vegetarian and likely halal, but contains gluten and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in cheesy sourdough bread?
Is sourdough bread good for diabetics?
What is the GI of sourdough bread?
Can I eat cheesy sourdough if I have high blood pressure?
What allergens are in cheesy sourdough?
How much sodium is in cheesy sourdough?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for golden cheesy sourdough exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheese bread and sourdough bread entries. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (sourdough wheat bread, GI=54) from the University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation rather than direct measurement.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheese bread (NDB 18963) and sourdough bread entries; no exact Singapore-specific entry was found in HPB Foodo. 2. Serving size of 120 g is estimated based on typical artisan bread slice portions; actual weight at Kazo Premium - Funan may differ. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheese bread source entry used for adaptation. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheese bread source entry used for adaptation. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheese bread source entry used for adaptation. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as bread and cheese products contain negligible vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central. 7. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate from sourdough wheat bread (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care / University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists. 8. Halal status is assumed likely but not confirmed; Kazo Premium halal certification status should be verified independently. 9. trans_fat_g of 0.1 reflects trace amounts typical of dairy-containing baked goods per USDA data.
